data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service experienced a significant increase in error rates, reaching 34.01%. This was primarily due to repeated Redis connection timeouts, affecting user session retrieval.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The qa-login service faced repeated timeouts when trying to retrieve user session data from the Redis database. The connection timed out after 5000ms, leading to a series of 'LoginException' errors.

evidenceChain: Increased error rate noted in Prometheus metric data, reaching 34.01 %
Loki logs showed frequent 'LoginException' errors due to Redis connection timeouts

timeline.event: First occurrence of 'LoginException' due to Redis connection timeout
Continued 'LoginException' errors due to Redis connection timeout
Error rate spikes to 34.01% as per Prometheus data

data.impact: Users were unable to log in as their session data could not be retrieved, significantly impacting user authentication processes.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Investigate and resolve the root cause of the Redis connection timeouts to prevent recurrence.
Consider restarting the Redis instance as done in incident KAN-6 to resolve any potentially orphaned connections.
Implement monitoring and alerts for Redis connection health to proactively address any degradations.
